markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (LOW) $147 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Game Handicap: NGX (-2.5) vs South America Rejects (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Grand final match between Nigma Galaxy and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 1 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy wins 3 or more games than South America Rejects in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "South America Rejects". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs South America Rejects (BO5) - DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Grand final match between Nigma Galaxy and South America Rejects in the DreamLeague Division 2 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 1 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against South America Rejects.

This market will resolve to "South America Rejects" if South America Rejects win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy enters the DreamLeague Division 2 Season 4 playoffs grand final BO5 with an impeccable undefeated run, topping Group A at 7-0 with a 14-0 map score before sweeping upper bracket foes Breeki Cheeki, Zero Tenacity, and 1w Team 2-0 each, showcasing dominant drafting and execution on patch 7.41b. South America Rejects, Group B winners at 6-1 (12-4 maps), mounted a gritty lower bracket charge after an early upper quarterfinal slip, dispatching ALIS VENTORUS, Team Lynx, Zero Tenacity, Power Rangers, and 1w Team—all 2-0 or 2-1—to force the decider. Both teams have secured DreamLeague Season 29 spots, leaving pride and $15,000 on the line; trader consensus likely favors NGX's momentum amid SAR's upset potential in extended series play.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Denver be 54°F or higher on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Buckley Space Force Base Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Buckley Space Force Base Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/co/aurora/KBKF.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest air temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C at the Observatory, driving trader consensus to 100% implied probability for 25°C or higher. This aligns with the exceptionally warm April conditions, featuring abundant sunshine under a stable high-pressure ridge and minimal cloud cover, consistent with late-spring subtropical climatology where mean maximum temperatures average 28°C. Historical data shows daily highs below 25°C are rare in late April, occurring less than 5% of the time since 1884. Only an unprecedented data revision from instrument malfunction—extremely unlikely given HKO's rigorous quality controls—could challenge this positioning, with final monthly summaries expected soon.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% in the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—its third straight hold—amid March CPI inflation surging to 3.3% year-over-year and resilient March nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs with unemployment steady at 4.3%. Officials highlighted elevated uncertainty from geopolitical tensions and sticky price pressures, tempering rate-cut expectations while dissenting votes signaled hawkish leanings. CME FedWatch implies a gradual rise toward 3.79% by late 2027, reflecting trader consensus on persistent inflation risks over rapid easing. Polymarket sentiment aggregates real-capital bets on whether the lower or upper bound hits specific levels by December 31, 2026. Key catalysts include April CPI on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.description:

A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Republican Party victory at 82% implied probability in the SC-02 House race, anchored by incumbent Rep. Joe Wilson's long tenure since 2001, his 59.7% 2024 general election margin, and the district's R+7 Cook Partisan Voting Index where Trump carried it by 14 points. Wilson's fundraising dominance—with $294,000 cash on hand as of March—far exceeds GOP primary challengers Hamp Redmond and Sam Gibbons ahead of the June 9 primaries. The Democratic field remains fragmented among David Robinson II, Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, and Daniel Shrief, contributing to the party's 18% odds. Candidate-specific markets for A (likely Wilson proxy), B, and Other near 50% reflect nominee uncertainty, with no polls or major developments in the past 30 days shifting dynamics; ratings hold at Solid Republican.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a high of 68-69°F (42% implied probability) for Los Angeles on May 2, driven by persistent marine layer clouds forecasted by the National Weather Service (NWS), which trap cool, moist Pacific air under a temperature inversion aloft—a hallmark of the "May Gray" pattern common in early May. This coastal cooling suppresses daytime heating despite typical seasonal averages around 72°F at LAX, with recent NWS Area Forecast Discussions noting patchy fog through midday and gradual clearing, limiting peaks to the upper 60s. NOAA's short-range models show continued onshore flow reinforcing this setup, though earlier burn-off could push toward 70-71°F (20.5%). Updated NWS guidance expected later today may refine these probabilities amid inherent forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors 180-199 posts by Elon Musk on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1 12:00 PM ET, reflecting the Polymarket live tracker's report of 184 total posts as of 5:20 AM ET on May 1 after seven days of activity.** This commanding position stems from Musk's steady daily average of 26 posts—including originals, replies, and quotes but excluding retweets and community reposts—without unusual spikes or lulls in the past week amid his routine commentary on SpaceX launches, xAI updates, and policy matters. With under seven hours remaining until resolution, the window for significant deviation has closed, as even accelerated posting would unlikely surpass 199. Only an unprecedented final-hour barrage exceeding 15 additional posts could challenge this outcome, though historical patterns show no such precedent in short intervals.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Bills hold a slim 55% trader consensus to repeat as AFC East champions, bolstered by Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and a draft class reinforcing their trenches with high-upside linemen like OT Caleb Lomu, following their consistent divisional dominance amid New England Patriots' 2025 playoff deep run to the AFC Championship. The Pats sit at 34.5% after Drake Maye's breakout sophomore campaign propelled their surprise division lead and Super Bowl berth loss, but offseason roster turnover and a tougher projected schedule temper expectations despite EDGE and TE additions. Jets (7.5%) and Dolphins (7.1%) trail amid Aaron Rodgers' age/injury risks and Tua Tagovailoa's fragility, with both teams' drafts yielding modest upgrades in a rebuilding phase.

markets.question: Will MrBeast say "Hundred" or "Thousand" or "Million" 5+ times during his next video?
markets.description:
MrBeast releases videos on https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mr Beast says the listed term during the next video he releases on YouTube. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old videos or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If no such video is posted by MrBeast by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be the released video from (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast).

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
markets.groupItemTitle: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Weibo Gaming, fueled by a 5-3 series record and perfect 2-0 head-to-head sweep this split—including a 2-1 win on April 25—positioning them 4th for direct playoff bracket qualification. AL rides a three-win streak highlighted by a 2-0 sweep over Invictus Gaming on April 28, with jungler Tarzan and top laner Flandre driving macro execution amid Fearless Draft constraints. Weibo Gaming, mired at 3-6 overall despite a recent 2-0 upset over Top Esports, leans on mid laner Xiaohu's experience and Jiejie's ganks but struggles with consistency; no injuries or roster shifts reported, though WBG fights to avoid play-in risks in the double round-robin.

markets.question: Spread: Gaziantep FK (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for May 1 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Gaziantep FK" if Gaziantep FK win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Beşiktaş JK".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on tff.org. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gaziantep FK vs. Beşiktaş JK - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Süper Lig game, scheduled for May 1 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Beşiktaş JK holds a commanding 4th place in the Süper Lig standings with 55 points from 30 matches, bolstered by scoring 10 goals across their last five games (three wins, including 4-2 over Antalyaspor), while Gaziantep FK languishes 10th on 37 points from 31 outings amid a winless streak in four of five (L-D-L-W-L). Both sides face injury woes—Gaziantep sidelined by long-term absences of wingers Yusuf Kabadayi and Ali Mevran Ablak plus right-back Salem M'Bakata (cruciate tears), and Beşiktaş without key midfielder Kartal Yılmaz (ankle ligaments) and Necip Uysal (muscle). Head-to-head tilts toward Beşiktaş (8-5-4), but Gaziantep's home form at Gaziantep Stadium and recent 2-2 draw offer upset potential amid European qualification stakes.

markets.question: Will Khvicha Kvaratskhelia be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent statements on April 30, 2026, threatening to withdraw U.S. troops from NATO bases in Italy and Spain—following similar warnings for Germany—have heightened tensions, stemming from European allies' refusal to support U.S. military actions in the Iran conflict. These remarks echo Secretary of State Marco Rubio's earlier criticisms of NATO as a "one-way street," amid demands for greater burden-sharing. However, full U.S. withdrawal under NATO Article 13 requires one year's notice and congressional approval per the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, posing significant barriers. Europe is advancing contingency plans to replace U.S. command, while traders weigh rhetorical pressure against institutional hurdles and no formal exit process initiated, with upcoming White House-NATO meetings potentially clarifying commitments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a high temperature of 21°C (77% implied probability) for May 2 in Wuhan, driven by the latest China Meteorological Administration (CMA) forecasts and global ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF, which project peaks in the 19–21°C range amid persistent cloud cover and moderate rainfall. This positioning reflects a cooler-than-average early May pattern, where seasonal norms hover around 25–26°C, but current synoptic conditions—including a weak frontal boundary—suppress daytime heating. The 17.6% odds on 22°C capture minor model spread and potential for partial clearing, while higher outcomes remain improbable given low wind shear and saturated low-level moisture. Final observational data from Wuhan Tianhe International Airport will resolve the market, with updated CMA bulletins expected within 24 hours.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 93% for direct North-South Korea talks by June 30, reflecting Pyongyang's entrenched hostility and prioritization of nuclear advancement over dialogue amid deepening military ties with Russia and China. Despite President Lee Jae-myung's April 6 expression of regret over civilian drone incursions—praised by Kim Jong Un and Kim Yo Jong as "wise"—North Korea responded with multiple ballistic missile tests on April 19 and 20, overseen by Kim, underscoring escalation rather than de-escalation. No high-level meetings have been proposed or scheduled, with Kim's March designation of South Korea as its "primary hostile state" and focus on weapons like Hwasong-11 series reinforcing preconditions unmet for talks. South Korea's unification minister sought EU mediation for "2+1" dialogue in early April, but Pyongyang's silence persists, leaving scant time for reversal absent major diplomatic breakthroughs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a high temperature of 62-63°F (56%) or 64-65°F (41%) in New York City on May 1, driven by the latest National Weather Service (NWS) guidance for Central Park, which projects highs near 62-66°F amid building high pressure and northwest winds capping warming. Morning observations show cool starts around 48-59°F with lingering fog and mist dissipating under mostly sunny to partly cloudy skies, moderated by recent cool air advection following frontal passages. Ensemble model runs from GFS and ECMWF show limited spread in the low-to-mid 60s, below the May 1 climatological normal of 68°F, with low shower risk potentially trimming peaks. Hourly NWS updates this afternoon will refine resolution as urban heat and boundary layer mixing play out.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' overwhelming early-season dominance in the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship has elevated teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus, with Russell's 40.5% implied probability edging the rookie's 32.7% amid their fiercely competitive intra-team duel. Antonelli leads the driver standings 72-63 after back-to-back victories in China and Japan, including pole over Russell in Suzuka, showcasing his one-lap pace and 88 laps led so far, while Russell's consistency keeps him close. Rivals like Charles Leclerc (49 points, 7.0%) and Oscar Piastri trail due to Mercedes' superior race pace and reliability through the first four Grands Prix, though McLaren and Ferrari upgrades loom ahead of Miami, sustaining the tight title fight.

markets.question: SV Werder Bremen vs. FC Augsburg: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game between SV Werder Bremen and FC Augsburg, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if SV Werder Bremen and FC Augsburg combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SV Werder Bremen vs. FC Augsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 2 at 9:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SV Werder Bremen, mired in 15th place on 28 points after 29 Bundesliga matches with a poor 7-7-15 record and -20 goal difference, desperately needs points from the May 2 home clash against 10th-placed FC Augsburg to avoid relegation. Breman's dismal form—losing over half their games—and defensive frailties are exacerbated by injuries to forward Victor Boniface (knee, fitness concerns), defender Mitchell Weiser (long-term), Felix Agu (muscle), and Karim Coulibaly (hamstring), plus potential suspensions like Cameron Puertas. Augsburg, more stable at 9-6-14, misses defenders Chrislain Matsima (hamstring, late April possible return) and Jeffrey Gouweleeuw (knee), but drew 0-0 with Bremen in December amid competitive head-to-heads (Bremen 11 wins, Augsburg 13). Home advantage at Weserstadion offers Bremen hope, though recent derby woes signal a tight contest.

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair before May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date range during which Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair expires on May 15, driving trader consensus to a 95.3% implied probability of his departure in the May 15–22 window, as he confirmed in his April 29 press conference—likely his last as chair—that he will step aside then while remaining on the Board of Governors through 2028. President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh advanced through the Senate Banking Committee on a party-line vote the same day, positioning a full Senate confirmation vote for mid-May to enable a seamless handover. This alignment of term end, official statements, and procedural progress solidifies the frontrunner, though delays in Senate confirmation, nomination withdrawal, or unforeseen legal challenges could push resolution later.

markets.question: Will Danny Musovski win the 2026 MLS Golden Boot?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 MLS Golden Boot.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLS rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLS season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Soccer; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Lionel Messi, Philip Zinckernagel, Denis Bouanga, and Petar Musa tightly around 40% implied probability for the 2026 MLS Golden Boot after roughly 10 matchdays, underscoring early-season parity in the race for regular-season top scorer. Musa leads outright with 9 goals for FC Dallas, matched by Sam Surridge's output for Nashville SC, while Messi's 7 goals in 9 games for Inter Miami highlight his clinical efficiency as the defending contender. Bouanga's recent hat-trick propelled LAFC's attack, and Zinckernagel's 4 goals—including a brace versus Sporting KC—signal breakout form at Chicago Fire, sustaining momentum amid favorable run-ins before the FIFA World Cup pause from late May. With 24 games remaining post-break, no player has distanced themselves, keeping the field competitive based on pace, historical 20-goal campaigns, and team firepower.

markets.question: Will Fenerbahçe Beko win the 2025-26 Euroleague?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club wins the 2025-2026 Euroleague season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to win the 2025-2026 Euroleague per the rules of the Euroleague (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Euroleague (https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olympiacos holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at 32% implied probability after surging to a 2-0 quarterfinal lead over AS Monaco, capped by a dominant 94-64 Game 2 rout at Peace and Friendship Stadium yesterday, showcasing superior defense and depth. Panathinaikos sits close at 27.5% following gritty road wins over Valencia Basket—68-67 in Game 1 and 107-105 in Game 2—highlighting their clutch play in tight spots. Fenerbahçe Beko (17.5%) and Real Madrid (16.0%) advanced with early advantages versus Žalgiris and Hapoel Tel Aviv, but face tougher paths amid best-of-five uncertainty. With the Final Four set for Athens' Telekom Center on May 22-24, Greek squads benefit from local support and momentum, keeping the race bunched as no series has reached three wins.

eventMetadata.context_description: San Lorenzo commands a slim 50.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite in this tightly contested LNB matchup against Obras Sanitarias, balancing Obras' superior 7th-place standing (20-14 record) and home-court edge with San Lorenzo's gritty road form despite their 15th position (13-22). The April 18 head-to-head, where Obras edged a 76-70 win via balanced scoring from Ty Sabin and Facundo Rutenberg, underscores the rivalry's closeness, amplified by both teams' middling recent results—Obras W-L-W-W-L, San Lorenzo struggling post-loss. Developments like official injury reports on stars such as Juan Brussino or San Lorenzo guards, lineup tweaks, or bench minutes distribution could tip odds amid late-season playoff pushes.
